Patch Set 2:

Maybe this could be split into 2 patches, one that would simply modify test-framework, the other would update sanity and sanity-hsm? The first one would break less often and probably land faster (plus other patches could be rebased on top of it quite easily).

Patch Set 2:

Quentin - I agree with you that reducing the number of changes would help this patch land faster. I do want to modify one test suite so that we can find any issues with the change now. So, I'll drop the changes to sanity-hsm and just modify sanity. Thanks.
Uploaded patch set 3.

I just wrote a script to help you find every occurence of "skip (...) return/exit"

#! /bin/bash
#

plop()
{
	local filepath="$1"
	local -i line_num=0

	[ -f "$filepath" ] || echo "error" >&2
	while read -r line; do
		line_num+=1
		[[ "$line" =~ skip[[:space:]]+ ]] || continue
		if [[ "$line" =~ return ]] || [[ "$line" =~ exit ]]; then
		       	printf '%s: l.%i: %s\n' "$filepath" "$line_num" "$line"
			continue
		fi
		read -r line
		[[ "$_line" =~ return ]] &&
		       	printf '%s: l.%i: %s\n%s\n' "$filepath" "$line_num" \
						    "$line" "$_line"
		line_num+=1
	done < "$filepath"
}

for arg; do
	plop "$arg"
done

There probably is a nice and robust way to do it with grep but I could not get my mind around it. =/

Hope this helps

commit.subject: LU-10461 tests: call exit in the skip routine
commit.message:
LU-10461 tests: call exit in the skip routine

There are many reasons to not run, or skip, a test; the test
may require a certain number of servers or a certain Lustre version.
In these cases, the skip() or skip_env() routine is called. When we
call skip, the intention is to exit the routine early. Thus, call
‘exit 0’ at the end of the skip() routine.

Some calls to skip() are changed to skip_env() when a test is being
skipped due to the Lustre configuration or test environment.

Signed-off-by: James Nunez <james.a.nunez@intel.com>
